Warning (extreme off-policy mismatch):

When ⟦INLMATH342MATHEND⟧ differs greatly from ⟦INLMATH343MATHEND⟧ — for example, when ⟦INLMATH344MATHEND⟧ has almost no samples in high-density regions of ⟦INLMATH345MATHEND⟧, or when ⟦INLMATH346MATHEND⟧ explodes in the tails — any ⟦INLMATH347MATHEND⟧-based method will suffer from severe variance issues. In such cases, the advantage of ⟦INLMATH348MATHEND⟧ (or ⟦INLMATH349MATHEND⟧) over ⟦INLMATH350MATHEND⟧ is no longer theoretically guaranteed, and strategies like clipping and regularization must be combined.

However, in RL practice we typically control KL constraints and limit the degree of off-policy sampling (e.g., using a nearby policy ⟦INLMATH351MATHEND⟧). In this common regime, we can say with confidence:

If you’ve decided to use importance sampling to optimize reverse KL, we recommend using ⟦INLMATH352MATHEND⟧ or ⟦INLMATH353MATHEND⟧ (both have equivalent gradients and low variance); in comparison, ⟦INLMATH354MATHEND⟧ has higher variance.

This is why the DeepSeek v3.2 technical report uses ⟦INLMATH355MATHEND⟧ as an off-policy KL penalty estimator.

Comprehensive Gradient Analysis Summary

Combining the above analysis, the following table summarizes the gradient expectations and corresponding optimization objectives for each estimator under the unified framework:

Sampling Type Loss Expected ⟦INLMATH356MATHEND⟧ Loss Optimization Objective Usable for Reverse KL?
on/off-policy ⟦INLMATH357MATHEND⟧ ⟦INLMATH358MATHEND⟧ Reverse KL ✓ (higher variance)
on/off-policy ⟦INLMATH359MATHEND⟧ ⟦INLMATH360MATHEND⟧ Surrogate (not reverse KL) ✗
on/off-policy ⟦INLMATH361MATHEND⟧ ⟦INLMATH362MATHEND⟧ Reverse KL ✓ (recommended, low variance)
on/off-policy ⟦INLMATH363MATHEND⟧ ⟦INLMATH364MATHEND⟧ Reverse KL ✓ (recommended, low variance)

where ⟦INLMATH365MATHEND⟧. When on-policy (⟦INLMATH366MATHEND⟧), ⟦INLMATH367MATHEND⟧.

It must be emphasized: the conclusions in the table above apply to the unified framework where “loss is written as ⟦INLMATH368MATHEND⟧ and ⟦INLMATH369MATHEND⟧ retains its gradient path in the computation graph”. In the on-policy case, although ⟦INLMATH370MATHEND⟧ numerically, since ⟦INLMATH371MATHEND⟧, we still have ⟦INLMATH372MATHEND⟧, so ⟦INLMATH373MATHEND⟧ and “directly backpropagating through the sample mean of ⟦INLMATH374MATHEND⟧” are not equivalent in terms of gradients.

If you use the naive on-policy implementation (i.e., after sampling from ⟦INLMATH375MATHEND⟧, treat ⟦INLMATH376MATHEND⟧ as ordinary scalars and directly backpropagate through their sample mean; without explicitly constructing ⟦INLMATH377MATHEND⟧ to restore the score-function path), then it degenerates to:

  • Directly using ⟦INLMATH378MATHEND⟧: ⟦INLMATH379MATHEND⟧ (ineffective)
  • Directly using ⟦INLMATH380MATHEND⟧: ⟦INLMATH381MATHEND⟧ (reverse KL) ✓
  • Directly using ⟦INLMATH382MATHEND⟧: ⟦INLMATH383MATHEND⟧ (forward KL) ✗

Key Conclusions:

  1. On-policy optimization of reverse KL (naive direct backprop implementation): The only correct choice is ⟦INLMATH384MATHEND⟧
  2. Off-policy optimization of reverse KL: Three correct options:
  • ⟦INLMATH385MATHEND⟧: Unbiased but higher variance
  • ⟦INLMATH386MATHEND⟧: Unbiased, gradient identical to ⟦INLMATH387MATHEND⟧
  • ⟦INLMATH388MATHEND⟧: Unbiased and lower variance (equivalent to the above, both recommended)
  1. ⟦INLMATH389MATHEND⟧ (weight participates in gradient) fails: This is an easily overlooked pitfall

7. Gradient Analysis When Used as a Reward Penalty

Having analyzed the gradient properties of the three estimators when used as loss, one might naturally think: since both ⟦INLMATH390MATHEND⟧ and ⟦INLMATH391MATHEND⟧ are unbiased for reverse KL value (see the “Value Estimation” section), using either of them (with stop-gradient) as a reward penalty should work fine.

But this conclusion is incomplete.

The issue is that when KL is used as a reward penalty, the KL term is detached, but it still influences the policy update through the advantage. Therefore, to decide whether an estimator is appropriate “in reward”, you must consider not only value bias, but whether the induced policy gradient is correct.

7.1 The True KL-Regularized Policy Gradient

Consider the KL-regularized reinforcement learning objective:

⟦DISPMATH48MATHEND⟧

Its true gradient is:

⟦DISPMATH49MATHEND⟧

Using the result from the “Preliminaries” section, the reverse KL gradient is:

⟦DISPMATH50MATHEND⟧

Therefore, the true KL-regularized policy gradient is:

⟦DISPMATH51MATHEND⟧

Gradient Form When Using Estimator ⟦INLMATH392MATHEND⟧

For the next few paragraphs, focus only on the policy-gradient term itself. I am not folding in extra effects from learned critics, GAE, baseline fitting error, or normalization. In that simplified setting, when we use some estimator ⟦INLMATH393MATHEND⟧ (with stop-gradient) as a reward penalty, the shaped reward is ⟦INLMATH394MATHEND⟧, and the policy gradient becomes:

⟦DISPMATH52MATHEND⟧

Unbiasedness condition: ⟦INLMATH395MATHEND⟧ if and only if

⟦DISPMATH53MATHEND⟧

Using ⟦INLMATH396MATHEND⟧ as Penalty: Gradient Unbiased

When ⟦INLMATH397MATHEND⟧, the condition is automatically satisfied:

⟦DISPMATH54MATHEND⟧

Therefore, when ⟦INLMATH398MATHEND⟧ is used as a reward penalty, the induced policy gradient is unbiased.

Using ⟦INLMATH399MATHEND⟧ as Penalty: Gradient Biased

When ⟦INLMATH400MATHEND⟧:

⟦DISPMATH55MATHEND⟧

The second term is exactly ⟦INLMATH401MATHEND⟧. The problem lies in the first term:

⟦DISPMATH56MATHEND⟧

This can be rewritten as:

⟦DISPMATH57MATHEND⟧

Using the forward KL gradient formula ⟦INLMATH402MATHEND⟧, we have:

⟦DISPMATH58MATHEND⟧

Therefore:

⟦DISPMATH59MATHEND⟧

When ⟦INLMATH403MATHEND⟧ is used as a reward penalty, the gradient is biased, with the bias term equal to the negative of the forward KL gradient.

Interpretation of the bias: Using ⟦INLMATH404MATHEND⟧ as a reward penalty is equivalent to optimizing a mixed objective that you likely do not intend:

  • Penalizing reverse KL (hoping policy doesn’t deviate from reference)
  • But also wrongly encouraging forward KL to increase (hoping reference doesn’t cover policy)

These two directions can conflict and destabilize optimization.

Empirical evidence: Shah et al. (2025) report that in on-policy RL fine-tuning of LLMs:

  • ⟦INLMATH405MATHEND⟧ in reward: Training is stable
  • ⟦INLMATH406MATHEND⟧ in reward: Training becomes unstable and can collapse

This is consistent with the theoretical analysis above.

Using ⟦INLMATH407MATHEND⟧ as Penalty: Also Biased

When ⟦INLMATH408MATHEND⟧, the KL part of the induced policy-gradient term becomes

⟦DISPMATH60MATHEND⟧

which is generally not equal to ⟦INLMATH409MATHEND⟧. So ⟦INLMATH410MATHEND⟧ is also biased when used inside reward shaping.

Off-Policy Scenario Conclusions

The above analysis assumes on-policy sampling. Does the conclusion change in off-policy scenarios?

Let samples come from behavior policy ⟦INLMATH411MATHEND⟧, using importance-weighted policy gradient:

⟦DISPMATH61MATHEND⟧

Using ⟦INLMATH412MATHEND⟧, this equals:

⟦DISPMATH62MATHEND⟧

The unbiasedness condition remains ⟦INLMATH413MATHEND⟧, exactly the same as on-policy.

Key insight: In an off-policy policy-gradient estimator, the importance weight ⟦INLMATH414MATHEND⟧ multiplies the entire policy-gradient term. There is no need to additionally importance-weight the KL scalar inside the shaped reward. Therefore:

  • Shaped reward keeps its original form: ⟦INLMATH415MATHEND⟧ (not ⟦INLMATH416MATHEND⟧)
  • Under the stop-gradient reward shaping (⟦INLMATH417MATHEND⟧) with the reverse-KL regularization setting discussed in this post, the conclusion is the same as in the on-policy case: use ⟦INLMATH418MATHEND⟧, not ⟦INLMATH419MATHEND⟧.

Note: This discussion assumes the usual current-sample / current-token reward-shaping form. In a general multi-step MDP, a fully rigorous off-policy derivation also needs per-step importance weighting or the corresponding value-function correction.

7.2 Key Finding: Under This Reward-Shaping Setup, Only ⟦INLMATH420MATHEND⟧ Keeps the Policy-Gradient Term Unbiased

Estimator Value unbiased? Policy-gradient term unbiased under stop-grad reward shaping? Actual performance
⟦INLMATH421MATHEND⟧ ✓ ✓ Stable
⟦INLMATH422MATHEND⟧ ✗ ✗ Not advised
⟦INLMATH423MATHEND⟧ ✓ ✗ Can collapse

Core lesson: “Value unbiasedness” and “gradient correctness” are independent axes. For the stop-gradient reward-shaping setup discussed here, only ⟦INLMATH424MATHEND⟧ gives the correct policy-gradient term for reverse-KL regularization. Even though ⟦INLMATH425MATHEND⟧ is value-unbiased and often lower variance, using it as a reward penalty introduces a biased update and may trigger collapse.

Scope reminder: once you add a learned critic, GAE, baseline normalization, and other implementation details, additional bias sources appear. The conclusion here is intentionally about the policy-gradient term itself.

At this point, an apparent tension may arise:

  • In reward penalty we emphasize “only use ⟦INLMATH426MATHEND⟧”;
  • But in the earlier loss-term backpropagation discussion (especially off-policy), we recommend using ⟦INLMATH427MATHEND⟧ or ⟦INLMATH428MATHEND⟧ for lower-variance reverse-KL gradients.

The next section explains why these are not contradictory: for the KL regularization term’s contribution to the policy-gradient update, the two implementations can be sample-wise equivalent. The practical differences arise mainly from whether the KL term enters the advantage/baseline and from the resulting credit-assignment pathway.

8. ⟦INLMATH429MATHEND⟧ in Reward vs. Low-Variance KL in Loss: Equivalence and Differences

Having separately analyzed KL as Loss and as Reward, a natural question arises: In what sense are these two approaches equivalent, and how do they differ? This section explores this question in depth, with particular focus on LLM RL practice.

8.1 Sample-Level Equivalence of the KL Gradient Term

This section compares only the policy-gradient contribution from KL regularization, written as the ascent direction ⟦INLMATH430MATHEND⟧ (minimizing a loss is just a global sign flip). We also keep the unified notation: samples come from ⟦INLMATH431MATHEND⟧, and the importance weight ⟦INLMATH432MATHEND⟧ multiplies the policy-gradient estimator.

Recall the key conclusions from earlier:

KL as Loss (low-variance choice): We proved earlier that when using ⟦INLMATH433MATHEND⟧ or ⟦INLMATH434MATHEND⟧ as the regularization term, the gradient random variable simplifies to

⟦DISPMATH63MATHEND⟧

KL as Reward (⟦INLMATH435MATHEND⟧ in reward): The shaped reward is ⟦INLMATH436MATHEND⟧ (applying stop-gradient to ⟦INLMATH437MATHEND⟧ just avoids “KL directly backpropagating” in implementation, without changing its numerical value as a penalty). In the “policy gradient term”, the KL penalty contributes

⟦DISPMATH64MATHEND⟧

Key finding: The KL gradient terms from both approaches are identical at the sample level.

In other words, ignoring the specific construction details of baseline/advantage:

  • “Writing KL into loss with low-variance implementation (⟦INLMATH438MATHEND⟧ or ⟦INLMATH439MATHEND⟧)”
  • and “Writing KL into reward with ⟦INLMATH440MATHEND⟧ (stop-gradient shaped reward)”

can exert exactly the same KL regularization “force” on policy updates.

Specifically, if we only look at the gradient term contributed by KL penalty when “maximizing ⟦INLMATH441MATHEND⟧” (the penalty term carries a negative sign in ⟦INLMATH442MATHEND⟧, so the ascent direction naturally carries ⟦INLMATH443MATHEND⟧):

  • KL in Loss (low-variance implementation): ⟦INLMATH444MATHEND⟧
  • KL in Reward (⟦INLMATH445MATHEND⟧ in reward): ⟦INLMATH446MATHEND⟧

They are the same random variable, not just equal in expectation.

Where the Two Implementations Still Differ

Although the KL gradient terms are sample-level equivalent, the overall update semantics of the two approaches still differ. The differences mainly manifest in the following aspects:

1. Whether KL Enters Advantage/Baseline

KL as Loss (equivalent to maximizing ⟦INLMATH447MATHEND⟧, but implementing the KL term as an independent, controllable “explicit force”):

⟦DISPMATH65MATHEND⟧

KL is an independent regularization term, completely decoupled from advantage. The magnitude of the KL gradient depends only on ⟦INLMATH448MATHEND⟧ itself, unaffected by critic quality or baseline choice.

KL as Reward:

⟦DISPMATH66MATHEND⟧

KL enters advantage computation through shaped reward and gets processed by the baseline. This means:

  • KL’s influence is modulated by how advantage is constructed
  • If using a value function baseline, KL’s influence is partially absorbed

From an implementation perspective, the difference can be understood as: the Loss approach estimates “environment return” and “KL regularization” separately; the Reward approach treats KL as part of the return, so it follows all the processing you do to returns (baseline, normalization, clipping, etc.).

2. Credit Assignment: Explicit Regularization vs. Shaped-Reward Coupling

KL as Loss: Each token/state’s KL gradient is local, directly affecting the update at that position.

KL as Reward: The KL penalty is folded into the return/advantage computation and can influence earlier decisions depending on how returns are propagated.

3. Reward-Centered KL: Impact on Gradient Unbiasedness

In LLM RL (such as GRPO, PPO for LLM), a common advantage computation is ⟦INLMATH449MATHEND⟧. When KL is used as a reward penalty, whether to include KL in the mean affects gradient unbiasedness.

Let samples be ⟦INLMATH450MATHEND⟧, denote ⟦INLMATH451MATHEND⟧, and use ⟦INLMATH452MATHEND⟧ for the KL penalty scalar of the ⟦INLMATH453MATHEND⟧-th sample, ⟦INLMATH454MATHEND⟧.

No centering (⟦INLMATH455MATHEND⟧): The expected KL gradient term is

⟦DISPMATH67MATHEND⟧

This is an unbiased gradient of ⟦INLMATH456MATHEND⟧.

Same-batch mean centering (⟦INLMATH457MATHEND⟧, including self): Since ⟦INLMATH458MATHEND⟧ depends on all samples (including ⟦INLMATH459MATHEND⟧ itself), the expected gradient becomes

⟦DISPMATH68MATHEND⟧

The KL regularization gradient is shrunk by ⟦INLMATH460MATHEND⟧, equivalent to a smaller effective ⟦INLMATH461MATHEND⟧. This is not strictly unbiased.

Leave-one-out centering (⟦INLMATH462MATHEND⟧): If we use ⟦INLMATH463MATHEND⟧ instead, then ⟦INLMATH464MATHEND⟧ is independent of ⟦INLMATH465MATHEND⟧, giving ⟦INLMATH466MATHEND⟧, therefore

⟦DISPMATH69MATHEND⟧

This remains an unbiased gradient, while enjoying variance reduction from centering.

Conclusion: Same-batch mean centering induces an ⟦INLMATH467MATHEND⟧ shrinkage of the KL gradient term (equivalently, a slight reduction in the effective ⟦INLMATH468MATHEND⟧). This is typically negligible for large group sizes (e.g., GRPO); for strict unbiasedness while retaining variance reduction, use a leave-one-out mean.

8.2 When to Choose Which Approach?

Dimension KL as Loss KL as Reward
KL gradient form ⟦INLMATH469MATHEND⟧ (low-var choice) ⟦INLMATH470MATHEND⟧
Coupling w/ Advantage Fully decoupled Coupled through shaped reward
KL centering None (absolute penalty) Yes (⟦INLMATH471MATHEND⟧)
Credit assignment Local, per-token May have temporal backprop (impl-dependent)
Suitable for Want KL as an explicit regularizer Want KL to flow through shaped reward / advantage

Practical recommendations:

  1. If you want KL to stay as an explicit regularizer — separate from advantage construction and less entangled with critic quality — choose KL as Loss, using ⟦INLMATH472MATHEND⟧ or ⟦INLMATH473MATHEND⟧. For on-policy scenarios, if you prefer not to explicitly construct ⟦INLMATH474MATHEND⟧, directly using ⟦INLMATH475MATHEND⟧ is simpler and less error-prone.

  2. If you want KL to be part of the shaped reward — so it flows through return / advantage construction together with the task reward — choose KL as Reward, using ⟦INLMATH476MATHEND⟧.

Based on the above conclusions about “value unbiasedness vs. gradient correctness” and “differences between Loss and Reward implementations”, we now proceed to the quick reference guide and common pitfalls that can be directly applied to code.

9. Practical Guide and Common Pitfalls

9.1 Quick Reference for the Three Estimator Definitions

⟦DISPMATH70MATHEND⟧

9.2 Value Estimation Properties

Estimator Unbiased for reverse KL ⟦INLMATH477MATHEND⟧ value? Variance
⟦INLMATH478MATHEND⟧ ✓ High (can be negative)
⟦INLMATH479MATHEND⟧ ✗ (often small near KL=0, not exact) Low
⟦INLMATH480MATHEND⟧ ✓ Low

9.3 Quick Reference Tables

On-Policy Optimization of Reverse KL (Loss)

Loss Form Pros Cons Rec.
⟦INLMATH481MATHEND⟧ — Gradient expectation is zero, completely ineffective ✗✗
⟦INLMATH482MATHEND⟧ Correct gradient (reverse KL), low variance, simplest implementation Value biased (but bias is minimal) ✓✓
⟦INLMATH483MATHEND⟧ — Gradient corresponds to forward KL, wrong direction ✗✗
⟦INLMATH484MATHEND⟧ Correct gradient (reverse KL), low variance, value unbiased Requires explicit ⟦INLMATH485MATHEND⟧ construction, slightly complex ✓

Note: ⟦INLMATH486MATHEND⟧ and ⟦INLMATH487MATHEND⟧ have identical gradients (sample-level equivalent). For on-policy, directly using ⟦INLMATH488MATHEND⟧ is recommended as the simplest approach.

Off-Policy Optimization of Reverse KL (Loss)

Loss Form Pros Cons Rec.
⟦INLMATH489MATHEND⟧ Correct gradient (reverse KL), value unbiased Higher variance △
⟦INLMATH490MATHEND⟧ — Gradient matches a local second-order surrogate, not reverse KL ✗✗
⟦INLMATH491MATHEND⟧ Correct gradient (reverse KL), low variance Value biased (but bias is minimal) ✓✓
⟦INLMATH492MATHEND⟧ Correct gradient (reverse KL), low variance, value unbiased — ✓✓

Note: ⟦INLMATH493MATHEND⟧ and ⟦INLMATH494MATHEND⟧ have identical gradients (sample-level equivalent). Both are recommended choices.

KL as Reward Penalty (stop-gradient shaped reward)

Estimator Pros Cons Rec.
⟦INLMATH495MATHEND⟧ Value unbiased, policy-gradient term unbiased Higher variance ✓✓
⟦INLMATH496MATHEND⟧ Value biased Policy-gradient term biased ✗✗
⟦INLMATH497MATHEND⟧ Value unbiased, low variance Policy-gradient term biased, bias term is ⟦INLMATH498MATHEND⟧, may cause training collapse ✗✗

Note: For the stop-gradient reward-shaping setup analyzed here, only ⟦INLMATH499MATHEND⟧ keeps the policy-gradient term aligned with reverse-KL regularization. Both ⟦INLMATH500MATHEND⟧ and ⟦INLMATH501MATHEND⟧ introduce bias in that term; ⟦INLMATH502MATHEND⟧ is especially treacherous because it looks attractive at the value-estimation level yet can still destabilize training.

Legend

  • ✓✓: Strongly recommended, theoretically correct with good practical performance
  • ✓: Recommended, theoretically correct but slightly more complex or has minor drawbacks
  • △: Usable but with caution, has issues like high variance
  • ✗✗: Not recommended for reverse KL; does not match the optimization objective discussed here or behaves poorly in practice

9.4 Common Pitfalls

  1. Using ⟦INLMATH503MATHEND⟧ directly as Loss (on-policy): Gradient expectation is zero, completely ineffective.
  2. Using ⟦INLMATH504MATHEND⟧ as Loss to optimize reverse KL (on-policy): Its gradient corresponds to forward KL ⟦INLMATH505MATHEND⟧, wrong direction.
  3. Using ⟦INLMATH506MATHEND⟧ (importance weight not detached) off-policy: Gradient corresponds to a local second-order surrogate, not reverse KL.
  4. Using ⟦INLMATH507MATHEND⟧ in reward penalty: Although value-unbiased, it induces a biased policy-gradient update and may lead to training collapse.
  5. Simply setting ⟦INLMATH508MATHEND⟧ to constant 1 in on-policy: Must explicitly construct ⟦INLMATH509MATHEND⟧ (or equivalently ⟦INLMATH510MATHEND⟧), otherwise the score-function gradient path is lost, causing ⟦INLMATH511MATHEND⟧ and ⟦INLMATH512MATHEND⟧ to degenerate to naive forms and fail.
  6. Confusing “value unbiasedness” with “gradient correctness”: ⟦INLMATH513MATHEND⟧ is value-unbiased for reverse KL, but when used as a reward penalty, the induced policy gradient is biased; both dimensions must be considered when choosing an estimator.

10. Summary

This post analyzes the three KL estimators ⟦INLMATH514MATHEND⟧ around three practical questions: who to sample from, how to use the estimator, and which quantity or gradient you actually care about.

Core takeaway: Value unbiasedness ≠ Gradient correctness. When choosing an estimator, you must consider both “whose value is being estimated” and “which optimization objective the gradient corresponds to”.

Key content:

  1. Value estimation: ⟦INLMATH515MATHEND⟧ and ⟦INLMATH516MATHEND⟧ are unbiased for reverse KL value, and ⟦INLMATH517MATHEND⟧ also has low variance.
  2. Gradients when used as Loss: Use ⟦INLMATH518MATHEND⟧ or ⟦INLMATH519MATHEND⟧ for on-policy; use ⟦INLMATH520MATHEND⟧ or ⟦INLMATH521MATHEND⟧ for off-policy.
  3. As a reward penalty: In the stop-gradient reward-shaping setup analyzed here, only ⟦INLMATH522MATHEND⟧ keeps the policy-gradient term aligned with reverse-KL regularization.
  4. Relationship between Loss and Reward implementations:
    • Sample-level equivalence: When Loss uses low-variance implementation (⟦INLMATH523MATHEND⟧ or ⟦INLMATH524MATHEND⟧) and Reward uses ⟦INLMATH525MATHEND⟧, their KL gradient terms are the same random variable ⟦INLMATH526MATHEND⟧ — not only equal in expectation, but also identical in variance.
    • Overall semantic differences: In the Loss approach, KL is an independent regularization term, completely decoupled from advantage, unaffected by critic quality; in the Reward approach, KL enters advantage computation through shaped reward and is processed and modulated by the baseline.
    • Credit assignment differences: The Loss approach’s KL gradient is local (per-token); the Reward approach’s KL penalty may propagate through returns to affect earlier decisions.
  5. Unified ⟦INLMATH527MATHEND⟧ framework: This post introduces ⟦INLMATH528MATHEND⟧ to treat on-policy and off-policy settings within a single framework. The key insight is that routing the sampling distribution’s ⟦INLMATH529MATHEND⟧-dependence through the ⟦INLMATH530MATHEND⟧ gradient path makes expect-then-differentiate and differentiate-then-expect coincide under ⟦INLMATH531MATHEND⟧. In on-policy settings, ⟦INLMATH532MATHEND⟧ but ⟦INLMATH533MATHEND⟧, which explains why directly backpropagating through ⟦INLMATH534MATHEND⟧ or ⟦INLMATH535MATHEND⟧ fails, while ⟦INLMATH536MATHEND⟧ works as a special case.

11. References

  1. Dibya Ghosh. “KL Divergence for Machine Learning”. https://dibyaghosh.com/blog/probability/kldivergence

  2. John Schulman. “Approximating KL Divergence”. https://joschu.net/blog/kl-approx.html

  3. Verl Documentation. “Proximal Policy Optimization (PPO)”. https://verl.readthedocs.io/en/latest/algo/ppo.html

  4. 初七123334. RLHF/RLVR 训练中的 KL 近似方法浅析（k1 / k2 / k3）. https://zhuanlan.zhihu.com/p/1966872846212010437

  5. Kezhao Liu, Jason Klein Liu, Mingtao Chen, Yiming Liu. “Rethinking KL Regularization in RLHF: From Value Estimation to Gradient Optimization”. https://arxiv.org/abs/2510.01555

  6. Yifan Zhang, Yiping Ji, Gavin Brown, et al. “On the Design of KL-Regularized Policy Gradient Algorithms for LLM Reasoning”. https://arxiv.org/abs/2505.17508

  7. Vedant Shah, Johan Obando-Ceron, Vineet Jain, Brian Bartoldson, Bhavya Kailkhura, Sarthak Mittal, Glen Berseth, Pablo Samuel Castro. “A Comedy of Estimators: On KL Regularization in RL Training of LLMs”. https://arxiv.org/abs/2512.21852

@misc{WangZhang2025KLEstimators,
  author       = {Wang, Xihuai and Zhang, Shao},
  title        = {Understanding KL Divergence Estimators in RL: From Numerical Estimation to Gradient Estimation},
  year         = {2025},
  month        = dec,
  day          = {01},
  url          = {https://xihuai18.github.io/reinforcement-learning/2025/12/01/kl-estimators-en.html}
}
entry.summary: Different KL estimators can behave quite differently in RL training. This post compares the classic estimators k1, k2, k3 in on-policy and off-policy settings, and explains how the choice changes when KL is used as a differentiable loss term versus a detached reward-shaping term.

entry.title: 简单理解 RL 中的 KL 散度估计器：从数值估计到梯度估计

link.title: 简单理解 RL 中的 KL 散度估计器：从数值估计到梯度估计

entry.content:
在强化学习中，不同 KL 估计器在训练里的行为可能差别很大。本文比较三种经典估计器 ⟦INLMATH71MATHEND⟧ 在 on-policy 和 off-policy 场景下的性质差异，并说明当 KL 作为可微 loss 或 detached 的奖励塑形项时，选型结论会怎样变化。

1. 引言：KL 散度（Kullback-Leibler 散度）在强化学习中的角色

在策略优化算法（如 PPO、GRPO）或对齐训练框架（如 RLHF、RLAIF）中，KL 惩罚项的作用很直接：限制当前策略不要离参考策略太远，从而降低训练不稳定甚至策略崩溃的风险。但一旦落实到代码，实现方式就会分出好几个维度：估计器的选择（⟦INLMATH72MATHEND⟧、⟦INLMATH73MATHEND⟧、⟦INLMATH74MATHEND⟧）、采样分布的选择（on-policy 或 off-policy），以及KL 项的用法（作为 loss 参与反向传播，还是作为奖励塑形项）。本文要做的，就是把这些选择之间的关系讲清楚。

尤其需要区分两件事：一是“估计某个 KL 的数值”，二是“反向传播时实际在优化哪个目标”。在很多实现里，这两者并不一致。

1.1 正向 KL 与反向 KL 的区别

设 ⟦INLMATH75MATHEND⟧ 为当前 actor 策略，⟦INLMATH76MATHEND⟧ 为参考策略，两种方向的 KL 散度分别为：

反向 KL（Reverse KL）：

⟦DISPMATH1MATHEND⟧

正向 KL（Forward KL）：

⟦DISPMATH2MATHEND⟧

直观解释：

  • 反向 KL 是 mode-seeking 的：优化后的策略会集中到参考分布的高概率区域，多样性可能降低。
  • 正向 KL 是 mass-covering 的：策略会尽量铺满参考分布的整个支撑集。

在 RLHF 的主流实现中，反向 KL更为常用，其原因在于我们通常期望 actor 策略不过度偏离参考策略，而非要求其完全覆盖参考分布的所有模式。

1.2 本文的核心问题：采样来源、估计目标与应用方式

在具体实现KL惩罚机制时，必须明确三个相互关联的核心问题：

  1. 采样来源：样本应来自当前策略 ⟦INLMATH77MATHEND⟧（同策略 on-policy），还是来自行为策略 ⟦INLMATH78MATHEND⟧（异策略 off-policy）？
  2. 估计目标：需要估计的是反向 KL 散度 ⟦INLMATH79MATHEND⟧，还是正向 KL 散度 ⟦INLMATH80MATHEND⟧？
  3. 应用方式：KL 项应作为损失函数的一部分参与反向传播，还是作为奖励塑形项（应用 stop-gradient 操作）？

这三个问题的不同组合会直接决定该选哪种估计器。本文会逐一分析这些组合，再给出可以直接落到实现里的选型建议。

2. 准备工作：符号与基本概念

在进入正文前，先统一符号，并把后文反复要用的两个基础结论写清楚。

2.1 符号、采样分布与解析梯度

符号约定

  • ⟦INLMATH81MATHEND⟧：当前 actor 策略（参数为 ⟦INLMATH82MATHEND⟧）
  • ⟦INLMATH83MATHEND⟧：若无歧义，后文简写 ⟦INLMATH84MATHEND⟧
  • ⟦INLMATH85MATHEND⟧：参考策略（reference policy），不依赖于 ⟦INLMATH86MATHEND⟧
  • ⟦INLMATH87MATHEND⟧：行为策略（behavior policy），用于 off-policy 采样，不依赖于 ⟦INLMATH88MATHEND⟧
  • ⟦INLMATH89MATHEND⟧：score function（得分函数）
  • ⟦INLMATH90MATHEND⟧：stop-gradient 操作（在代码中对应 .detach()）

统一的采样策略视角：引入 ⟦INLMATH91MATHEND⟧ 记号

分析 KL 估计器的梯度性质时，on-policy 和 off-policy 看起来需要分开处理，但其实可以放进同一个框架。

为此，我们引入采样策略 ⟦INLMATH92MATHEND⟧，即数据来源于分布 ⟦INLMATH93MATHEND⟧，并定义统一的重要性权重比率：

⟦DISPMATH3MATHEND⟧

此定义的关键在于：无论同策略还是异策略场景，我们都将采样策略 ⟦INLMATH94MATHEND⟧ 视为梯度常数（即对 ⟦INLMATH95MATHEND⟧ 应用stop-gradient操作）。

  • 异策略（Off-policy）场景（⟦INLMATH96MATHEND⟧）：由于 ⟦INLMATH97MATHEND⟧ 本身不依赖于 ⟦INLMATH98MATHEND⟧，故 ⟦INLMATH99MATHEND⟧，此时 ⟦INLMATH100MATHEND⟧。
  • 同策略（On-policy）场景（⟦INLMATH101MATHEND⟧）：令 ⟦INLMATH102MATHEND⟧ 但对其应用stop-gradient操作，则 ⟦INLMATH103MATHEND⟧（数值恒为1），但 ⟦INLMATH104MATHEND⟧。

实现注意事项：在同策略情况下，尽管数值上 ⟦INLMATH105MATHEND⟧，但仍要在计算图中显式构造 ⟦INLMATH106MATHEND⟧（或等价写成 ⟦INLMATH107MATHEND⟧）。如果直接把 ⟦INLMATH108MATHEND⟧ 写成常数 1，score function 这条梯度路径就会丢掉，推导也会退化成后文说的“朴素同策略实现”。

核心洞察：⟦INLMATH109MATHEND⟧ 补上的，是“采样分布对参数 ⟦INLMATH110MATHEND⟧ 的依赖”这条梯度路径。On-policy 时，“先取期望后求梯度”和“先求梯度后取期望”之所以不一样，就是因为少了这条路径；显式构造 ⟦INLMATH111MATHEND⟧，就是把它补回来。

有了这个记号，后面的推导就不需要再分 on-policy 和 off-policy 两套写法了。

得分函数与KL散度的解析梯度

得分函数具有一个重要性质：⟦INLMATH112MATHEND⟧（由 ⟦INLMATH113MATHEND⟧ 可得）。

基于这一性质，我们可以推导正向与反向KL散度关于参数 ⟦INLMATH114MATHEND⟧ 的解析梯度。

反向 KL 的梯度：

⟦DISPMATH4MATHEND⟧

对 ⟦INLMATH115MATHEND⟧ 求梯度（应用乘积法则）：

⟦DISPMATH5MATHEND⟧

利用 ⟦INLMATH116MATHEND⟧，以及 ⟦INLMATH117MATHEND⟧、⟦INLMATH118MATHEND⟧：

⟦DISPMATH6MATHEND⟧

即：

⟦DISPMATH7MATHEND⟧

注：后文将定义 ⟦INLMATH119MATHEND⟧，因此上式可简写为 ⟦INLMATH120MATHEND⟧，这一形式将在后续梯度分析中反复出现。

正向KL散度的梯度：

⟦DISPMATH8MATHEND⟧

⟦DISPMATH9MATHEND⟧

为使用来自 ⟦INLMATH123MATHEND⟧ 的样本估计该梯度，我们引入重要性采样技术：

其中最后一步用到了 ⟦INLMATH124MATHEND⟧。因此

注：后文将推导 ⟦INLMATH125MATHEND⟧，因此 ⟦INLMATH126MATHEND⟧（正向KL散度）——这解释了为何直接对 ⟦INLMATH127MATHEND⟧ 进行反向传播会产生「错误」的梯度方向。

基于这两个结果，我们能够在后续分析中判断各估计器的梯度期望分别对应何种KL散度的解析梯度。

3. 三种估计器的定义与设计原理

基于概率比值 ⟦INLMATH128MATHEND⟧，John Schulman提出了三种单样本估计器。本节将详细介绍这些估计器的定义及其设计原理。

3.1 三种估计器：定义与直观解释

⟦INLMATH129MATHEND⟧：最朴素的 log-ratio 估计器

⟦DISPMATH12MATHEND⟧

这是最直观的定义——直接取对数比值的负值。它对反向 KL 是无偏的，但存在一个致命缺陷：估计值可能为负数，而 KL 散度始终是非负的。这会导致方差极高，因为正负估计值会相互抵消。

⟦INLMATH130MATHEND⟧：基于平方 log-ratio 的估计器

⟦DISPMATH13MATHEND⟧

设计动机：⟦INLMATH131MATHEND⟧ 的估计值可正可负，而 ⟦INLMATH132MATHEND⟧ 通过取平方确保每个样本的估计值都非负，从而每个样本都能直观地衡量 ⟦INLMATH133MATHEND⟧ 和 ⟦INLMATH134MATHEND⟧ 之间的差异程度。

为什么偏差较小？ 更准确地说，⟦INLMATH135MATHEND⟧ 不是严格意义上的标准 f-散度，因此这里最稳妥的说法不是“它本身是某个 f-散度”，而是“它在 ⟦INLMATH136MATHEND⟧ 的邻域里与 KL 具有相同的二阶局部行为”。更具体地，若取 ⟦INLMATH137MATHEND⟧ 使得 ⟦INLMATH138MATHEND⟧，并在标准正则性条件下（以保证积分与微分可交换）对参数做小扰动 ⟦INLMATH139MATHEND⟧，则有

⟦DISPMATH14MATHEND⟧

同时

⟦DISPMATH15MATHEND⟧

其中 ⟦INLMATH140MATHEND⟧ 是 ⟦INLMATH141MATHEND⟧ 处的 Fisher 信息矩阵。也就是说，当策略足够接近时，⟦INLMATH142MATHEND⟧ 与真实 KL 散度共享相同的局部曲率，偏差主要来自更高阶项；一旦离开小-KL 邻域，这个偏差就不一定仍然很小。

⟦INLMATH143MATHEND⟧：控制变量法构造的 Bregman 散度估计器

⟦DISPMATH16MATHEND⟧

设计动机：我们希望得到一个既无偏又低方差的估计器。标准做法是为 ⟦INLMATH144MATHEND⟧ 添加一个控制变量（control variate）——即期望为零但与 ⟦INLMATH145MATHEND⟧ 负相关的量。

注意到 ⟦INLMATH146MATHEND⟧，因此对于任意 ⟦INLMATH147MATHEND⟧，

⟦DISPMATH17MATHEND⟧

仍然是无偏估计。

为什么选择 ⟦INLMATH148MATHEND⟧？ 由于 ⟦INLMATH149MATHEND⟧ 是凹函数，有 ⟦INLMATH150MATHEND⟧，因此

⟦DISPMATH18MATHEND⟧

始终非负！这保证了每个样本都「正向」贡献信息，避免了 ⟦INLMATH151MATHEND⟧ 中正负估计值相互抵消的问题。

几何视角：⟦INLMATH152MATHEND⟧ 实际上是一个 Bregman 散度。考虑凸函数 ⟦INLMATH153MATHEND⟧，它在 ⟦INLMATH154MATHEND⟧ 处的切线为 ⟦INLMATH155MATHEND⟧。Bregman 散度定义为函数值与切线值之差：

⟦DISPMATH19MATHEND⟧

由于凸函数始终位于其切线上方，该差值自然非负。更重要的是，当 ⟦INLMATH156MATHEND⟧ 时，函数与切线「贴合」得越来越紧密，差值以 ⟦INLMATH157MATHEND⟧ 的二阶速度趋近于零——这正是 ⟦INLMATH158MATHEND⟧ 在策略接近时方差较小的根本原因。

小结：三者的设计逻辑对比

估计器 定义 设计原理
⟦INLMATH159MATHEND⟧ ⟦INLMATH160MATHEND⟧ 最朴素定义
⟦INLMATH161MATHEND⟧ ⟦INLMATH162MATHEND⟧ 局部二阶行为与 KL 一致的 surrogate
⟦INLMATH163MATHEND⟧ ⟦INLMATH164MATHEND⟧ 控制变量 + Bregman 散度

在明确了三种估计器的定义与设计原理之后，我们首先分析它们在估计KL散度数值时的性质，即偏差与方差特性。

4. 数值估计：偏差与方差

本节将分析三种估计器在估计KL散度数值时的性质。这些性质构成了一切使用场景的基础。

假设从 ⟦INLMATH165MATHEND⟧ 采样来估计反向 KL ⟦INLMATH166MATHEND⟧：

4.1 无偏性分析

⟦DISPMATH20MATHEND⟧

结论：在估计反向 KL 的数值时，⟦INLMATH167MATHEND⟧ 和 ⟦INLMATH168MATHEND⟧ 是无偏估计，而 ⟦INLMATH169MATHEND⟧ 是有偏估计。

4.2 方差特性分析

John Schulman 的实验（⟦INLMATH170MATHEND⟧，⟦INLMATH171MATHEND⟧，真实 KL = 0.005）显示：

估计器 偏差/真值 标准差/真值
⟦INLMATH172MATHEND⟧ 0 20
⟦INLMATH173MATHEND⟧ 0.002 1.42
⟦INLMATH174MATHEND⟧ 0 1.42

当 KL 较大时（⟦INLMATH175MATHEND⟧，真实 KL = 0.5）：

估计器 偏差/真值 标准差/真值
⟦INLMATH176MATHEND⟧ 0 2
⟦INLMATH177MATHEND⟧ 0.25 1.73
⟦INLMATH178MATHEND⟧ 0 1.7

核心直观理解：

  • ⟦INLMATH179MATHEND⟧ 以一阶项起步，当 ⟦INLMATH180MATHEND⟧ 接近 1 时波动较大，且可能取负值
  • ⟦INLMATH181MATHEND⟧ 在 ⟦INLMATH182MATHEND⟧ 处是二阶小量，始终非负，因此在策略接近时方差较小
  • 但当覆盖严重不足（⟦INLMATH183MATHEND⟧ 可能极大）时，⟦INLMATH184MATHEND⟧ 的方差会因权重爆炸而增大；此时 ⟦INLMATH185MATHEND⟧ 反而更加稳定

数值估计小结

估计器 对数值的偏差 方差特性
⟦INLMATH186MATHEND⟧ 无偏 高（可正可负）
⟦INLMATH187MATHEND⟧ 有偏（小 KL 邻域通常较小） 低（恒正）
⟦INLMATH188MATHEND⟧ 无偏 低（恒正）

从数值估计的角度看，在 小 KL、覆盖良好 的常见局部场景下，⟦INLMATH189MATHEND⟧ 往往是「无偏 + 低方差」的更稳妥选择。

注：若要估计正向 KL 的数值 ⟦INLMATH190MATHEND⟧，且只能从 ⟦INLMATH191MATHEND⟧ 采样，则可以使用重要性采样 ⟦INLMATH192MATHEND⟧。

5. KL 惩罚的两种使用方式

接下来的问题是：KL 惩罚在实现里到底怎么用？ 这会决定我们是只关心估计器的数值性质，还是还得把梯度性质一并算进去。

回顾 KL 正则化强化学习的目标函数（下式中 ⟦INLMATH193MATHEND⟧ 表示“由策略 ⟦INLMATH194MATHEND⟧ 诱导的轨迹分布”）：

⟦DISPMATH21MATHEND⟧

这个数学形式看似统一，但在基于策略梯度（Policy Gradient）的算法（如 PPO）中实现时，却衍生出两种截然不同的实现范式——它们在代码层面可能只差几行，却对应着完全不同的优化语义。

符号说明：本节用 ⟦INLMATH195MATHEND⟧ 或 ⟦INLMATH196MATHEND⟧ 泛指某个 token/state 级的 KL 估计器（如 ⟦INLMATH197MATHEND⟧），具体定义见前文「三种估计器的定义与设计原理」一节。

5.1 作为 Loss：KL 参与梯度反传

actor_loss = -advantage * log_prob + beta * kl  # kl 参与梯度计算

Critic 仅学习环境价值，KL 作为 actor 的正则项直接参与 loss 的反向传播。

5.2 作为 Reward：KL 加入奖励塑形

kl = compute_kl(log_prob_q, log_prob_p).detach()
shaped_reward = reward - beta * kl

KL 被视为环境奖励的一部分，使用形塑后的奖励进行标准的 actor-critic 更新。KL 项本身不参与 loss 的反向传播。

这两种做法看似只是代码中一个 .detach() 的区别，实际上对应着截然不同的优化语义。两种方式的深入对比将在后文「⟦INLMATH198MATHEND⟧ 的 Reward 用法与低方差 KL Loss 用法的等价性与差异」一节详细展开。此处先给出核心区别：

  • KL 作为 Loss：需要 KL 估计器的正确显式梯度，关心梯度对应哪个优化目标
  • KL 作为 Reward：需要 KL 的准确数值估计，同时还要关注它诱导的策略梯度是否正确

下面我们按照「作为 Loss」和「作为 Reward」两种使用方式，深入剖析估计器的梯度性质。

6. 作为 Loss 时的梯度分析

当 KL 散度作为损失函数参与反向传播时，不同估计器对应的优化目标并不相同。这也是实践里最容易踩坑的地方。

借助前文引入的统一框架，我们可以将 on-policy 与 off-policy 场景下的分析合并为一套推导。回顾统一的比率定义：

⟦DISPMATH22MATHEND⟧

其中 ⟦INLMATH199MATHEND⟧ 为采样策略。在此框架下：

  • On-policy（⟦INLMATH200MATHEND⟧）：⟦INLMATH201MATHEND⟧，但 ⟦INLMATH202MATHEND⟧
  • Off-policy（⟦INLMATH203MATHEND⟧）：⟦INLMATH204MATHEND⟧，且 ⟦INLMATH205MATHEND⟧

6.1 三种估计器的基本梯度

首先计算三种估计器本身的梯度（不含 ⟦INLMATH206MATHEND⟧），这些结果将在后续分析中反复使用。

推导 ⟦INLMATH207MATHEND⟧：

⟦DISPMATH23MATHEND⟧

⟦DISPMATH24MATHEND⟧

推导 ⟦INLMATH208MATHEND⟧：

⟦DISPMATH25MATHEND⟧

由链式法则：

⟦DISPMATH26MATHEND⟧

推导 ⟦INLMATH209MATHEND⟧：

⟦DISPMATH27MATHEND⟧

首先计算 ⟦INLMATH210MATHEND⟧。由于 ⟦INLMATH211MATHEND⟧：

⟦DISPMATH28MATHEND⟧

再计算 ⟦INLMATH212MATHEND⟧：

⟦DISPMATH29MATHEND⟧

因此：

⟦DISPMATH30MATHEND⟧

小结：三种估计器的梯度分别为：

  • ⟦INLMATH213MATHEND⟧
  • ⟦INLMATH214MATHEND⟧
  • ⟦INLMATH215MATHEND⟧

这些基本梯度将在后续的统一框架分析中反复使用。

「先期望后梯度」vs「先梯度后期望」：一个重要警示

在分析 KL 估计器的梯度时，有一个容易混淆的陷阱：「先期望后梯度」与「先梯度后期望」可能给出不同的结果。

如果从解析角度将 ⟦INLMATH216MATHEND⟧ 视为 ⟦INLMATH217MATHEND⟧ 的函数再求梯度（即「先期望后梯度」），根据「数值估计」一节的结论 ⟦INLMATH218MATHEND⟧，我们有：

⟦DISPMATH31MATHEND⟧

⟦DISPMATH32MATHEND⟧

两者都给出反向 KL 的梯度。然而，在代码中直接对 ⟦INLMATH219MATHEND⟧ 的样本均值进行反向传播时，自动微分执行的是「先梯度后期望」，得到 ⟦INLMATH220MATHEND⟧——这与「先期望后梯度」的结果可能不同。

这种差异的根源在于：当采样分布 ⟦INLMATH221MATHEND⟧ 本身依赖于 ⟦INLMATH222MATHEND⟧ 时，期望与梯度不能随意交换。这正是 on-policy 场景的核心困难，也是我们需要引入统一 ⟦INLMATH223MATHEND⟧ 框架的原因。

6.2 统一框架下的梯度分析

现在，我们使用 ⟦INLMATH224MATHEND⟧ 框架统一处理 on-policy 和 off-policy 场景。考虑损失函数形式 ⟦INLMATH225MATHEND⟧，其中 ⟦INLMATH226MATHEND⟧。

关键观察：由于 ⟦INLMATH227MATHEND⟧ 不依赖于 ⟦INLMATH228MATHEND⟧，对于任何关于 ⟦INLMATH229MATHEND⟧ 可微的函数 ⟦INLMATH230MATHEND⟧，有

⟦DISPMATH33MATHEND⟧

这意味着在 ⟦INLMATH231MATHEND⟧ 框架下，「先期望后梯度」与「先梯度后期望」总是等价的——无论 on-policy 还是 off-policy。

注意：这里的“期望”指的是对固定的采样分布 ⟦INLMATH232MATHEND⟧ 的 ⟦INLMATH233MATHEND⟧。我们将“分布对 ⟦INLMATH234MATHEND⟧ 的依赖”统一纳入 ⟦INLMATH235MATHEND⟧ 这条路径中；因此不要将这句话误读为对 ⟦INLMATH236MATHEND⟧ 也能无条件地交换微分与期望。

统一框架下三种估计器的梯度推导

利用 ⟦INLMATH237MATHEND⟧（因为 ⟦INLMATH238MATHEND⟧），结合前文推导的 ⟦INLMATH239MATHEND⟧，应用乘积法则：

⟦INLMATH240MATHEND⟧：

⟦DISPMATH34MATHEND⟧

⟦INLMATH241MATHEND⟧：

⟦DISPMATH35MATHEND⟧

⟦INLMATH242MATHEND⟧（对 ⟦INLMATH243MATHEND⟧ 施加 stop-gradient）：

⟦DISPMATH36MATHEND⟧

⟦INLMATH244MATHEND⟧：

⟦DISPMATH37MATHEND⟧

代入 ⟦INLMATH245MATHEND⟧：

⟦DISPMATH38MATHEND⟧

因此得到一个关键简化：

⟦DISPMATH39MATHEND⟧

梯度期望与优化目标

利用 ⟦INLMATH246MATHEND⟧ 和 ⟦INLMATH247MATHEND⟧：

⟦INLMATH248MATHEND⟧：

⟦DISPMATH40MATHEND⟧

⟦INLMATH249MATHEND⟧：

⟦DISPMATH41MATHEND⟧

也就是说，⟦INLMATH250MATHEND⟧ 的梯度期望对应的是“最小化 ⟦INLMATH251MATHEND⟧”（一个与 KL 具有相同局部二阶行为的 surrogate），而不是反向 KL ⟦INLMATH252MATHEND⟧ 的解析梯度；因此当目标是精确优化反向 KL 时，不宜直接使用 ⟦INLMATH253MATHEND⟧。

⟦INLMATH254MATHEND⟧：

⟦DISPMATH42MATHEND⟧

⟦INLMATH255MATHEND⟧：

⟦DISPMATH43MATHEND⟧

梯度等价性：哪些方法产生相同的梯度随机变量

从上述推导中，我们发现一个关键事实：

⟦INLMATH256MATHEND⟧ 与 ⟦INLMATH257MATHEND⟧ 的梯度完全相同：⟦INLMATH258MATHEND⟧

这意味着它们不仅期望相同，而且作为随机变量完全等价——同均值、同方差、同高阶矩。

总结表格：

Loss 形式 梯度随机变量 梯度期望 对应的优化目标
⟦INLMATH259MATHEND⟧ ⟦INLMATH260MATHEND⟧ ⟦INLMATH261MATHEND⟧ 反向 KL ✓
⟦INLMATH262MATHEND⟧ ⟦INLMATH263MATHEND⟧ ⟦INLMATH264MATHEND⟧ 局部二阶 surrogate（非反向 KL）✗
⟦INLMATH265MATHEND⟧ ⟦INLMATH266MATHEND⟧ ⟦INLMATH267MATHEND⟧ 反向 KL ✓
⟦INLMATH268MATHEND⟧ ⟦INLMATH269MATHEND⟧ ⟦INLMATH270MATHEND⟧ 反向 KL ✓

6.3 On-policy 与 Off-policy 的统一视角

现在，我们可以通过统一框架重新审视 on-policy 与 off-policy 的关系。

On-policy（⟦INLMATH271MATHEND⟧）：

  • ⟦INLMATH272MATHEND⟧（数值恒为 1）
  • ⟦INLMATH273MATHEND⟧，⟦INLMATH274MATHEND⟧，⟦INLMATH275MATHEND⟧
  • 但梯度不同！因为 ⟦INLMATH276MATHEND⟧

这解释了为什么 on-policy 时朴素直接反向传播（不显式构造 ⟦INLMATH277MATHEND⟧）使用 ⟦INLMATH278MATHEND⟧ 或 ⟦INLMATH279MATHEND⟧ 作为损失函数会出问题：

  • 直接使用 ⟦INLMATH280MATHEND⟧：相当于没有 ⟦INLMATH281MATHEND⟧ 的版本，⟦INLMATH282MATHEND⟧，完全无效
  • 直接使用 ⟦INLMATH283MATHEND⟧：相当于没有 ⟦INLMATH284MATHEND⟧ 的版本，⟦INLMATH285MATHEND⟧（正向 KL），方向错误
  • 直接使用 ⟦INLMATH286MATHEND⟧：⟦INLMATH287MATHEND⟧（反向 KL）✓ 在这种朴素实现下是与目标一致的选择

但如果显式构造 ⟦INLMATH288MATHEND⟧，则：

  • 可用：⟦INLMATH289MATHEND⟧（方差高）、⟦INLMATH290MATHEND⟧（推荐）、⟦INLMATH291MATHEND⟧（推荐）——三者均给出反向 KL 梯度
  • 不宜直接用于精确优化反向 KL：⟦INLMATH292MATHEND⟧（⟦INLMATH293MATHEND⟧ 参与梯度）——优化的是与 KL 局部二阶行为一致的 surrogate，而非反向 KL

Off-policy（⟦INLMATH294MATHEND⟧）：

  • ⟦INLMATH295MATHEND⟧（标准重要性权重）
  • 可用：⟦INLMATH296MATHEND⟧（方差高）、⟦INLMATH297MATHEND⟧（推荐）、⟦INLMATH298MATHEND⟧（推荐）——三者均给出反向 KL 梯度
  • 不宜直接用于精确优化反向 KL：⟦INLMATH299MATHEND⟧（⟦INLMATH300MATHEND⟧ 参与梯度）——优化的是与 KL 局部二阶行为一致的 surrogate，而非反向 KL

关键洞察：on-policy 时 ⟦INLMATH301MATHEND⟧ 能直接工作，本质上是因为 ⟦INLMATH302MATHEND⟧ 的梯度形式 ⟦INLMATH303MATHEND⟧ 恰好等于 ⟦INLMATH304MATHEND⟧（当 ⟦INLMATH305MATHEND⟧ 时）。这是一个「巧合」，而非一般规律。

关于大模型 off-policy 场景的深入分析，可以参考我之前的博客：从两策略到三策略：LLM RL 中行为策略–参考策略不一致下的 TRPO 扩展。

6.4 方差分析

前面我们看到，给出反向 KL 无偏梯度的有三个选择：⟦INLMATH306MATHEND⟧、⟦INLMATH307MATHEND⟧、⟦INLMATH308MATHEND⟧。它们的梯度随机变量分别为（注意 ⟦INLMATH309MATHEND⟧ 是向量，因此梯度也是向量）：

⟦DISPMATH44MATHEND⟧

其中 ⟦INLMATH310MATHEND⟧ 对应 ⟦INLMATH311MATHEND⟧ 和 ⟦INLMATH312MATHEND⟧（两者完全相同）。

为了避免“向量梯度的方差”这一表述的歧义，我们比较任意方向上的投影方差：取任意单位向量 ⟦INLMATH313MATHEND⟧，定义标量随机变量

⟦DISPMATH45MATHEND⟧

令 ⟦INLMATH314MATHEND⟧，⟦INLMATH315MATHEND⟧，则

⟦DISPMATH46MATHEND⟧

两者期望相同，且任意方向上的方差之差为

⟦DISPMATH47MATHEND⟧

（你也可以将此理解为对每个坐标分量分别比较方差；结论与直观量级判断一致。）

在典型的 KL 惩罚场景下（⟦INLMATH316MATHEND⟧），取 ⟦INLMATH317MATHEND⟧，⟦INLMATH318MATHEND⟧：

  • ⟦INLMATH319MATHEND⟧
  • ⟦INLMATH320MATHEND⟧，主导项为正的 ⟦INLMATH321MATHEND⟧ 常数

因此在足够小的邻域里，⟦INLMATH322MATHEND⟧。

一旦离开这个小-KL 邻域，⟦INLMATH323MATHEND⟧ 的符号就不再固定；这时整体比较还要结合 ⟦INLMATH324MATHEND⟧ 与 score 项的加权来判断，不能再简单依赖这一局部展开。

核心直观理解：

  • ⟦INLMATH325MATHEND⟧ 包含一个量级为 ⟦INLMATH326MATHEND⟧ 的零均值噪声项 ⟦INLMATH327MATHEND⟧
  • ⟦INLMATH328MATHEND⟧ 已将该常数噪声项消去，剩下与 ⟦INLMATH329MATHEND⟧ 成正比的一阶小量

方差对比表格：

估计器 梯度随机变量 系数量级（⟦INLMATH330MATHEND⟧） 方差
⟦INLMATH331MATHEND⟧ ⟦INLMATH332MATHEND⟧ ⟦INLMATH333MATHEND⟧ 高
⟦INLMATH334MATHEND⟧ ⟦INLMATH335MATHEND⟧ ⟦INLMATH336MATHEND⟧ 低
⟦INLMATH337MATHEND⟧ ⟦INLMATH338MATHEND⟧ ⟦INLMATH339MATHEND⟧ 低

结论：⟦INLMATH340MATHEND⟧ 与 ⟦INLMATH341MATHEND⟧ 在梯度层面完全等价——同均值、同方差、同高阶矩；相比之下，⟦INLMATH342MATHEND⟧ 的梯度多了一个零均值的常数噪声项，在典型的小-KL 场景下其方差通常更高。

实践建议：若优化反向 KL，首选 ⟦INLMATH343MATHEND⟧ 或 ⟦INLMATH344MATHEND⟧（两者梯度等价且方差低）；⟦INLMATH345MATHEND⟧ 虽无偏但方差高，可作为备选并需配合 clipping/正则化。

极度 off-policy 时的警示：

当 ⟦INLMATH346MATHEND⟧ 与 ⟦INLMATH347MATHEND⟧ 差异很大时——例如 ⟦INLMATH348MATHEND⟧ 在 ⟦INLMATH349MATHEND⟧ 的高密度区域几乎没有采样，或 ⟦INLMATH350MATHEND⟧ 在尾部爆炸——任何基于 ⟦INLMATH351MATHEND⟧ 的方法都会遭遇严重的方差问题。此时，⟦INLMATH352MATHEND⟧（或 ⟦INLMATH353MATHEND⟧）相对于 ⟦INLMATH354MATHEND⟧ 的优势不再有理论保证，需要结合 clipping、正则化等策略综合处理。

不过，在 RL 实践中，我们通常会控制 KL 约束、限制 off-policy 程度（例如使用近邻策略 ⟦INLMATH355MATHEND⟧）。在这个常见的场景中，可以相当有信心地说：

如果已经决定用重要性采样来优化反向 KL，推荐使用 ⟦INLMATH356MATHEND⟧ 或 ⟦INLMATH357MATHEND⟧（两者梯度等价且方差低）；相较之下，⟦INLMATH358MATHEND⟧ 方差更高。

与本文的分析一致，DeepSeek-V3.2 技术报告中使用的是 ⟦INLMATH359MATHEND⟧ 作为 off-policy KL 惩罚的估计器。

梯度分析总览表

综合以上分析，下表汇总了统一框架下各估计器的梯度期望及其对应的优化目标：

采样类型 Loss ⟦INLMATH360MATHEND⟧ Loss 的期望 对应的优化目标 能否用于优化反向 KL？
on/off-policy ⟦INLMATH361MATHEND⟧ ⟦INLMATH362MATHEND⟧ 反向 KL ✓（但方差较高）
on/off-policy ⟦INLMATH363MATHEND⟧ ⟦INLMATH364MATHEND⟧ 局部二阶 surrogate（非反向 KL） ✗
on/off-policy ⟦INLMATH365MATHEND⟧ ⟦INLMATH366MATHEND⟧ 反向 KL ✓（推荐，低方差）
on/off-policy ⟦INLMATH367MATHEND⟧ ⟦INLMATH368MATHEND⟧ 反向 KL ✓（推荐，低方差）

其中 ⟦INLMATH369MATHEND⟧。当 on-policy（⟦INLMATH370MATHEND⟧）时，⟦INLMATH371MATHEND⟧。

需要特别强调：上表的结论针对的是 “loss 写成 ⟦INLMATH372MATHEND⟧ 且 ⟦INLMATH373MATHEND⟧ 在计算图中保留梯度路径” 的统一框架。在 on-policy 时，虽然数值上 ⟦INLMATH374MATHEND⟧，但由于 ⟦INLMATH375MATHEND⟧，仍有 ⟦INLMATH376MATHEND⟧，因此 ⟦INLMATH377MATHEND⟧ 与“直接对 ⟦INLMATH378MATHEND⟧ 的样本均值反向传播”在梯度上并不等价。

如果你采用的是朴素 on-policy 写法（即从 ⟦INLMATH379MATHEND⟧ 采样后，将 ⟦INLMATH380MATHEND⟧ 视为普通标量，对其样本均值直接反向传播；不显式构造 ⟦INLMATH381MATHEND⟧ 来补上 score-function 路径），则会退化为：

  • 直接使用 ⟦INLMATH382MATHEND⟧：⟦INLMATH383MATHEND⟧（无效）
  • 直接使用 ⟦INLMATH384MATHEND⟧：⟦INLMATH385MATHEND⟧（反向 KL）✓
  • 直接使用 ⟦INLMATH386MATHEND⟧：⟦INLMATH387MATHEND⟧（正向 KL）✗

关键结论：

  1. On-policy 优化反向 KL（朴素直接反向传播的实现）：在本文限定的实现方式下，⟦INLMATH388MATHEND⟧ 是与目标最一致的选择
  2. Off-policy 优化反向 KL：有三个正确选项：
    • ⟦INLMATH389MATHEND⟧：无偏但方差较高
    • ⟦INLMATH390MATHEND⟧：无偏，与 ⟦INLMATH391MATHEND⟧ 梯度完全等价
    • ⟦INLMATH392MATHEND⟧：无偏且方差更低（与上一项等价，均为推荐选择）
  3. ⟦INLMATH393MATHEND⟧（权重参与梯度）不对应本文目标：它优化的是一个与 KL 仅在局部二阶行为上一致的 surrogate，而不是反向 KL；这是一个容易被忽视的陷阱

7. 作为 Reward 时的梯度分析

前文分析的是 KL 作为 loss 时各估计器的梯度性质。一个自然的想法是：既然 ⟦INLMATH394MATHEND⟧ 和 ⟦INLMATH395MATHEND⟧ 对反向 KL 的数值估计都是无偏的（见「数值估计」一节），那把它们加上 stop-gradient 之后放进奖励塑形里，似乎也应该没问题。

但这个判断还不够充分。

问题在于：当 KL 进入奖励塑形时，虽然 KL 项本身不反向传播梯度，但它会通过 advantage 间接影响策略梯度。因此，判断一个估计器能不能这样用，不能只看数值偏差，还要看它诱导出来的策略梯度是否正确。

7.1 真正的 KL 正则化策略梯度

考虑 KL 正则化的强化学习目标：

⟦DISPMATH48MATHEND⟧

其解析梯度为：

⟦DISPMATH49MATHEND⟧

利用前文「准备工作」章节的结论，反向 KL 的梯度为：

⟦DISPMATH50MATHEND⟧

因此，真正的 KL 正则化策略梯度是：

⟦DISPMATH51MATHEND⟧

使用估计器 ⟦INLMATH396MATHEND⟧ 时的梯度形式

下文只讨论策略梯度主项本身，暂不把 baseline、critic 拟合误差、GAE 与额外归一化一并纳入。此时，如果把某个估计器 ⟦INLMATH397MATHEND⟧（加 stop-gradient）放进奖励塑形，形塑后的奖励为 ⟦INLMATH398MATHEND⟧，策略梯度变为：

⟦DISPMATH52MATHEND⟧

在这个主项分析下，无偏条件是：⟦INLMATH399MATHEND⟧ 当且仅当

⟦DISPMATH53MATHEND⟧

使用 ⟦INLMATH400MATHEND⟧ 作为惩罚：梯度无偏

当 ⟦INLMATH401MATHEND⟧ 时，条件自动满足：

⟦DISPMATH54MATHEND⟧

因此，就该策略梯度主项而言，把 ⟦INLMATH402MATHEND⟧ 放进奖励塑形时诱导的是无偏梯度。

使用 ⟦INLMATH403MATHEND⟧ 作为惩罚：梯度有偏

当 ⟦INLMATH404MATHEND⟧ 时：

⟦DISPMATH55MATHEND⟧

第二项正是 ⟦INLMATH405MATHEND⟧。问题出在第一项：

⟦DISPMATH56MATHEND⟧

而这个量可以改写为：

⟦DISPMATH57MATHEND⟧

利用正向 KL 的梯度公式 ⟦INLMATH406MATHEND⟧，有：

⟦DISPMATH58MATHEND⟧

因此：

⟦DISPMATH59MATHEND⟧

把 ⟦INLMATH407MATHEND⟧ 放进奖励塑形时，梯度是有偏的，偏差项等于正向 KL 梯度的负值。

偏差的几何含义：把 ⟦INLMATH408MATHEND⟧ 放进奖励塑形，相当于在优化一个「错误的混合目标」：

  • 既惩罚反向 KL（希望策略不偏离参考）
  • 又错误地鼓励正向 KL 变大（希望参考不覆盖策略）

这两个方向相互冲突，可能导致优化不稳定。

实验现象：Shah et al. (2025) 的实验表明，在其 on-policy LLM-RL 设定下：

  • ⟦INLMATH409MATHEND⟧ in reward：训练稳定
  • ⟦INLMATH410MATHEND⟧ in reward：出现显著不稳定，甚至训练崩溃

这与本文给出的偏差分析方向一致。

使用 ⟦INLMATH411MATHEND⟧ 作为惩罚：同样有偏

当 ⟦INLMATH412MATHEND⟧ 时，奖励塑形项对应的梯度项为

⟦DISPMATH60MATHEND⟧

它一般不等于 ⟦INLMATH413MATHEND⟧。因此，把 ⟦INLMATH414MATHEND⟧ 放进奖励塑形时同样会诱导有偏的策略梯度。

Off-policy 场景下的结论

上述分析假设 on-policy 采样。在 off-policy 场景下，结论是否改变？

设样本来自行为策略 ⟦INLMATH415MATHEND⟧，使用重要性加权的策略梯度：

⟦DISPMATH61MATHEND⟧

利用 ⟦INLMATH416MATHEND⟧，上式等于：

⟦DISPMATH62MATHEND⟧

无偏条件仍然是 ⟦INLMATH417MATHEND⟧，与 on-policy 完全相同。

关键洞察：在本文关注的当前样本 / 当前 token 级 off-policy 策略梯度主项里，重要性权重 ⟦INLMATH418MATHEND⟧ 作用于整个策略梯度估计器，不需要再对 shaped reward 中的 KL 估计器额外乘一层权重。因此：

  • Shaped reward 保持原形式：⟦INLMATH419MATHEND⟧（不是 ⟦INLMATH420MATHEND⟧）
  • 在本文讨论的 stop-grad reward shaping（⟦INLMATH421MATHEND⟧）且目标为 反向 KL 正则 的设定下：结论与 on-policy 的策略梯度主项相同，只有 ⟦INLMATH422MATHEND⟧ 能保持无偏的梯度主项

注：这里默认的是当前样本/当前 token 级的 reward shaping 写法；若回到一般多步 MDP 的严格 off-policy 推导，还需要配合逐步重要性采样或相应的值函数修正。

7.2 关键发现：在本文分析的策略梯度主项里，只有 ⟦INLMATH423MATHEND⟧ 能保持无偏梯度

估计器 数值无偏？ 作为 Reward 惩罚时梯度主项无偏？ 实际表现
⟦INLMATH424MATHEND⟧ ✓ ✓ 稳定
⟦INLMATH425MATHEND⟧ ✗ ✗ 不建议
⟦INLMATH426MATHEND⟧ ✓ ✗ 显著不稳定

核心教训：评价 KL 估计器时，「数值无偏」和「梯度正确」是两个独立的维度。对于本文讨论的奖励塑形用法（stop-grad reward shaping，目标为反向 KL 正则；无论 on-policy 还是 off-policy），就策略梯度主项而言，只有 ⟦INLMATH427MATHEND⟧ 满足目标要求。⟦INLMATH428MATHEND⟧ 虽然数值无偏且方差更低，但放进奖励塑形后会导致梯度有偏，实践中可能显著不稳定甚至崩溃。

补充说明：一旦把 learned critic、GAE、baseline 归一化等实现细节一并纳入，实际更新中的偏差分析会更复杂。本节结论刻意只聚焦于 policy-gradient 主项，以避免把不同来源的偏差混在一起。

到这里容易产生一个“表面矛盾”：

  • 在 Reward 惩罚里我们强调“若目标是本文讨论的 reverse-KL stop-grad shaping，则只有 ⟦INLMATH429MATHEND⟧ 在策略梯度主项上无偏”；
  • 但在前文 Loss 反传（尤其 off-policy）里，我们又推荐用 ⟦INLMATH430MATHEND⟧ 或 ⟦INLMATH431MATHEND⟧ 来获得更低方差的反向 KL 梯度。

下一节将解释：两者并不冲突——就“KL 正则项对应的 policy-gradient 随机变量”而言，它们甚至可以做到样本级完全等价；差异主要来自 KL 是否进入 advantage/baseline、以及信用分配（credit assignment）的路径。

8. ⟦INLMATH432MATHEND⟧ 的 Reward 用法与低方差 KL Loss 用法的等价性与差异

前面我们分别分析了 KL 作为 Loss 和作为 Reward 两种使用方式。一个自然的问题是：这两种方式在什么意义上等价，又在什么意义上不同？ 本节将深入探讨这个问题，特别是在大模型 RL 的实践场景下。

8.1 KL 梯度项的样本级等价性

本节只比较“KL 正则化带来的那一项策略梯度”，并统一写成 policy gradient 的上升方向 ⟦INLMATH433MATHEND⟧（若你在代码里最小化 loss，则整体只差一个全局负号，不影响等价性结论）。同时默认你使用的是本文前文的统一权重记号：样本来自 ⟦INLMATH434MATHEND⟧，重要性权重 ⟦INLMATH435MATHEND⟧ 作用在策略梯度估计器上。

回顾前文的关键结论：

KL 作为 Loss（低方差选择）：前文已证明，采用 ⟦INLMATH436MATHEND⟧ 或 ⟦INLMATH437MATHEND⟧ 作为正则项时，梯度随机变量都化简为

⟦DISPMATH63MATHEND⟧

KL 作为 Reward（⟦INLMATH438MATHEND⟧ in reward）：shaped reward 为 ⟦INLMATH439MATHEND⟧（对 ⟦INLMATH440MATHEND⟧ 做 stop-gradient 只是在实现上避免“KL 直接反传”，不改变它作为惩罚的数值）。在“策略梯度项”里，KL 惩罚贡献的是

⟦DISPMATH64MATHEND⟧

关键发现：两者的 KL 梯度项样本级完全相同。

也就是说，在不考虑 baseline/advantage 的具体构造细节时：

  • “把 KL 写进 loss 并用低方差实现（⟦INLMATH441MATHEND⟧ 或 ⟦INLMATH442MATHEND⟧）”
  • 与“把 KL 写进 reward 并选 ⟦INLMATH443MATHEND⟧（stop-grad shaped reward）”

对策略更新施加的 KL 正则“力”可以是一模一样的。

具体来说，如果我们只看“最大化 ⟦INLMATH444MATHEND⟧”时 KL 惩罚贡献的那一项梯度（惩罚项在 ⟦INLMATH445MATHEND⟧ 里带负号，因此这项的上升方向自然带 ⟦INLMATH446MATHEND⟧）：

  • KL in Loss（低方差实现）：⟦INLMATH447MATHEND⟧
  • KL in Reward（⟦INLMATH448MATHEND⟧ in reward）：⟦INLMATH449MATHEND⟧

它们是同一个随机变量，不仅期望相同，方差也完全相同。

整体更新语义的差异

尽管 KL 梯度项在样本级等价，两种方式的整体更新语义仍然不同。差异主要体现在以下几个方面：

1. KL 是否进入 Advantage/Baseline

KL 作为 Loss（等价于最大化 ⟦INLMATH450MATHEND⟧，但把 KL 项作为一个独立的、可控的“显式力”来实现）：

⟦DISPMATH65MATHEND⟧

KL 是一个独立的正则项，与 advantage 完全解耦。KL 梯度的大小只取决于 ⟦INLMATH451MATHEND⟧ 本身，不受 critic 质量或 baseline 选择的影响。

KL 作为 Reward：

⟦DISPMATH66MATHEND⟧

KL 通过 shaped reward 进入 advantage 计算，会被 baseline 处理。这意味着：

  • KL 的影响会被 advantage 的构造方式调制
  • 如果使用 value function baseline，KL 的影响会被部分吸收

从实现角度看，这里的差别可以理解为：Loss 方案把“环境回报部分”和“KL 正则部分”分开估计；Reward 方案把 KL 视为回报的一部分，因此它会跟着你对回报做的所有处理（baseline、归一化、截断等）一起走。

2. 信用分配：独立正则力 vs 混入 Shaped Reward

KL 作为 Loss：每个 token/state 的 KL 梯度是「局部」的，只影响该位置的策略更新。

KL 作为 Reward：KL 惩罚通过 return/advantage 的时间回传，可能影响到更早的决策。

3. Reward 中心化 KL：对梯度无偏性的影响

在大模型 RL（如 GRPO、PPO for LLM）中，常见的 advantage 计算方式是 ⟦INLMATH452MATHEND⟧。当 KL 作为 Reward 时，是否把 KL 也纳入 mean 会影响梯度的无偏性。

设采样 ⟦INLMATH453MATHEND⟧，记 ⟦INLMATH454MATHEND⟧，并用 ⟦INLMATH455MATHEND⟧ 表示第 ⟦INLMATH456MATHEND⟧ 个样本的 KL 惩罚标量，⟦INLMATH457MATHEND⟧。

不中心化（⟦INLMATH458MATHEND⟧）：KL 梯度项的期望为

⟦DISPMATH67MATHEND⟧

这是对 ⟦INLMATH459MATHEND⟧ 的无偏梯度。

同 batch 均值中心化（⟦INLMATH460MATHEND⟧，含自身）：由于 ⟦INLMATH461MATHEND⟧ 依赖所有样本（包括 ⟦INLMATH462MATHEND⟧ 自身），期望梯度变为

⟦DISPMATH68MATHEND⟧

即 KL 正则梯度被缩小了 ⟦INLMATH463MATHEND⟧，等价于有效 ⟦INLMATH464MATHEND⟧ 变小。这不是严格无偏的。

Leave-one-out 中心化（⟦INLMATH465MATHEND⟧）：若改用 ⟦INLMATH466MATHEND⟧，则 ⟦INLMATH467MATHEND⟧ 与 ⟦INLMATH468MATHEND⟧ 独立，有 ⟦INLMATH469MATHEND⟧，因此

⟦DISPMATH69MATHEND⟧

仍是无偏梯度，同时享受中心化带来的方差缩减。

结论：同 batch 均值中心化引入的偏差为 ⟦INLMATH470MATHEND⟧，在 GRPO 等大 batch 场景下影响很小；若追求严格无偏，可改用 leave-one-out 均值，同时享受方差缩减。

8.2 何时选择哪种方式？

维度 KL 作为 Loss KL 作为 Reward
KL 梯度形态 ⟦INLMATH471MATHEND⟧（低方差选择） ⟦INLMATH472MATHEND⟧
与 Advantage 完全解耦 通过 shaped reward 耦合
KL 中心化 无（绝对惩罚） 有（⟦INLMATH473MATHEND⟧）
信用分配 局部、per-token 可能有时间回传（取决于实现）
适用场景 希望 KL 作为显式正则项单独控制 希望 KL 随 shaped reward 一起进入 advantage / return

实践建议：

  1. 如果你希望 KL 作为显式正则项单独控制——也就是尽量少受 advantage 构造、critic / baseline 质量影响，那么请选择 KL 作为 Loss，使用 ⟦INLMATH474MATHEND⟧ 或 ⟦INLMATH475MATHEND⟧。注意，在 on-policy 场景下，如果不想显式构造 ⟦INLMATH476MATHEND⟧，直接使用 ⟦INLMATH477MATHEND⟧ 会更简单且不易出错。

  2. 如果你希望 KL 随 shaped reward 一起进入 advantage / return——也就是接受它与 baseline、信用分配路径耦合，那么请选择 KL 作为 Reward，并使用 ⟦INLMATH478MATHEND⟧。

基于上述“数值无偏 vs 梯度正确”以及“Loss 与 Reward 实现差异”的结论，下面进入可直接照抄到代码里的选型速查与常见踩坑点。

9. 实践指南与常见陷阱

9.1 三种估计器定义速查

⟦DISPMATH70MATHEND⟧

9.2 数值估计性质

估计器 对反向 KL ⟦INLMATH479MATHEND⟧ 数值无偏？ 方差
⟦INLMATH480MATHEND⟧ ✓ 高（估计值可负）
⟦INLMATH481MATHEND⟧ ✗（小 KL 邻域通常偏差较小） 低
⟦INLMATH482MATHEND⟧ ✓ 低

9.3 选型速查表

On-policy 优化反向 KL（Loss）

Loss 形式 优点 问题 推荐
⟦INLMATH483MATHEND⟧ — 梯度期望为零，完全无效，不能用于优化 ✗✗
⟦INLMATH484MATHEND⟧ 梯度正确（反向 KL），低方差，实现最简单 数值有偏（小 KL 邻域通常偏差较小） ✓✓
⟦INLMATH485MATHEND⟧ — 若目标是反向 KL，则它对应的是正向 KL 梯度 ✗✗
⟦INLMATH486MATHEND⟧ 梯度正确（反向 KL），低方差，数值无偏 需显式构造 ⟦INLMATH487MATHEND⟧，实现稍复杂 ✓

注：⟦INLMATH488MATHEND⟧ 与 ⟦INLMATH489MATHEND⟧ 的梯度完全相同（样本级等价）。On-policy 时推荐直接用 ⟦INLMATH490MATHEND⟧，实现最简单。

Off-policy 优化反向 KL（Loss）

Loss 形式 优点 问题 推荐
⟦INLMATH491MATHEND⟧ 梯度正确（反向 KL），数值无偏 方差较高 △
⟦INLMATH492MATHEND⟧ — 梯度对应局部二阶 surrogate（非反向 KL），与本文目标不匹配 ✗✗
⟦INLMATH493MATHEND⟧ 梯度正确（反向 KL），低方差 数值有偏（小 KL 邻域通常偏差较小） ✓✓
⟦INLMATH494MATHEND⟧ 梯度正确（反向 KL），低方差，数值无偏 — ✓✓

注：⟦INLMATH495MATHEND⟧ 与 ⟦INLMATH496MATHEND⟧ 的梯度完全相同（样本级等价）。两者均为推荐选择。

KL 作为 Reward 惩罚（stop-grad shaped reward）

估计器 优点 问题 推荐
⟦INLMATH497MATHEND⟧ 数值无偏，诱导的梯度主项无偏 方差较高 ✓✓
⟦INLMATH498MATHEND⟧ 数值有偏 诱导的策略梯度一般有偏（通常不等于目标项） ✗✗
⟦INLMATH499MATHEND⟧ 数值无偏，低方差 诱导的策略梯度有偏，偏差项为 ⟦INLMATH500MATHEND⟧，实践中可能显著不稳定甚至崩溃 ✗✗

注：在本文讨论的 stop-grad reward shaping 场景下，只有 ⟦INLMATH501MATHEND⟧ 能保持与反向 KL 正则一致的无偏策略梯度主项。⟦INLMATH502MATHEND⟧ 和 ⟦INLMATH503MATHEND⟧ 都会引入偏差；其中 ⟦INLMATH504MATHEND⟧ 虽然数值无偏且方差低，但实践中曾观察到显著不稳定甚至崩溃。

图例说明

  • ✓✓：强烈推荐，理论正确且实践表现好
  • ✓：推荐，理论正确但实现稍复杂或有小缺点
  • △：可用但需谨慎，存在方差高等问题
  • ✗✗：与本文当前优化目标不匹配，不建议这样用

9.4 常见陷阱

  1. On-policy 下使用 ⟦INLMATH505MATHEND⟧ 作为 Loss：梯度期望为零，完全无效。
  2. On-policy 下使用 ⟦INLMATH506MATHEND⟧ 作为 Loss 来优化反向 KL：若目标是反向 KL，其梯度实际对应的是正向 KL ⟦INLMATH507MATHEND⟧。
  3. Off-policy 下使用 ⟦INLMATH508MATHEND⟧（重要性权重不 detach）：梯度对应一个与 KL 仅在局部二阶行为上一致的 surrogate，而非反向 KL。
  4. 在 Reward 惩罚中使用 ⟦INLMATH509MATHEND⟧：虽然数值无偏，但诱导的策略梯度有偏，实践中可能显著不稳定甚至崩溃。
  5. On-policy 时将 ⟦INLMATH510MATHEND⟧ 简单地设为常数 1：必须显式构造 ⟦INLMATH511MATHEND⟧（或等价地 ⟦INLMATH512MATHEND⟧），否则会丢失 score-function 梯度路径，使 ⟦INLMATH513MATHEND⟧ 和 ⟦INLMATH514MATHEND⟧ 退化为朴素形式，从而不再对应前文的无偏结论。
  6. 混淆「数值无偏」与「梯度正确」：⟦INLMATH515MATHEND⟧ 对反向 KL 数值无偏，但作为 Reward 惩罚时诱导的策略梯度有偏；选择估计器时必须同时考虑这两个维度。

10. 总结

一句话概括：选 KL 估计器时，“数值无偏”和“梯度正确”是两回事，必须结合具体用法分别判断。

核心结论：数值无偏 ≠ 梯度正确。选择估计器时，必须同时考虑「估计谁的数值」和「梯度对应哪个优化目标」。

核心内容：

  1. 数值估计：⟦INLMATH516MATHEND⟧ 和 ⟦INLMATH517MATHEND⟧ 对反向 KL 数值无偏；在小 KL、覆盖良好的常见局部场景下，⟦INLMATH518MATHEND⟧ 往往还兼具较低方差。
  2. 作为 Loss 时的梯度：在 on-policy 场景下使用 ⟦INLMATH519MATHEND⟧ 或 ⟦INLMATH520MATHEND⟧；在 off-policy 场景下使用 ⟦INLMATH521MATHEND⟧ 或 ⟦INLMATH522MATHEND⟧。
  3. 作为 Reward 惩罚：在本文讨论的 stop-grad reward shaping 的策略梯度主项里，只有使用 ⟦INLMATH523MATHEND⟧ 才能保持与反向 KL 正则一致的无偏梯度。
  4. Loss 与 Reward 两种实现的关系：
    • 样本级等价性：就 KL 正则项对应的 policy-gradient 随机变量而言，当 Loss 使用低方差实现（⟦INLMATH524MATHEND⟧ 或 ⟦INLMATH525MATHEND⟧）、Reward 使用 ⟦INLMATH526MATHEND⟧ 时，两者的 KL 梯度项是同一个随机变量 ⟦INLMATH527MATHEND⟧，不仅期望相同，方差也完全相同。
    • 整体语义差异：在 Loss 方式中，KL 是独立的正则项，与 advantage 完全解耦，不受 critic 质量的影响；而在 Reward 方式中，KL 通过 shaped reward 进入 advantage 计算，会被 baseline 处理和调制。
    • 信用分配差异：Loss 方式的 KL 梯度是局部的（per-token）；而 Reward 方式的 KL 惩罚可能通过 return 回传影响更早的决策。
  5. 统一 ⟦INLMATH528MATHEND⟧ 框架：本文引入 ⟦INLMATH529MATHEND⟧ 来统一处理 on-policy 和 off-policy 场景。该框架的核心洞察是：将「采样分布对 ⟦INLMATH530MATHEND⟧ 的依赖」显式地纳入 ⟦INLMATH531MATHEND⟧ 这条梯度路径，从而使「先期望后梯度」与「先梯度后期望」在 ⟦INLMATH532MATHEND⟧ 下总是等价。在 on-policy 场景中，⟦INLMATH533MATHEND⟧ 但 ⟦INLMATH534MATHEND⟧，这解释了为什么直接对 ⟦INLMATH535MATHEND⟧ 或 ⟦INLMATH536MATHEND⟧ 进行反向传播不再对应前文的统一无偏结论，而 ⟦INLMATH537MATHEND⟧ 能「巧合」地工作。

11. 参考文献

  1. Dibya Ghosh. “KL Divergence for Machine Learning”. https://dibyaghosh.com/blog/probability/kldivergence

  2. John Schulman. “Approximating KL Divergence”. https://joschu.net/blog/kl-approx.html

  3. Verl Documentation. “Proximal Policy Optimization (PPO)”. https://verl.readthedocs.io/en/latest/algo/ppo.html

  4. 初七123334. “RLHF/RLVR 训练中的 KL 近似方法浅析（k1 / k2 / k3）”. https://zhuanlan.zhihu.com/p/1966872846212010437

  5. Kezhao Liu, Jason Klein Liu, Mingtao Chen, Yiming Liu. “Rethinking KL Regularization in RLHF: From Value Estimation to Gradient Optimization”. https://arxiv.org/abs/2510.01555

  6. Yifan Zhang, Yiping Ji, Gavin Brown, et al. “On the Design of KL-Regularized Policy Gradient Algorithms for LLM Reasoning”. https://arxiv.org/abs/2505.17508

  7. Vedant Shah, Johan Obando-Ceron, Vineet Jain, Brian Bartoldson, Bhavya Kailkhura, Sarthak Mittal, Glen Berseth, Pablo Samuel Castro. “A Comedy of Estimators: On KL Regularization in RL Training of LLMs”. https://arxiv.org/abs/2512.21852

@misc{WangZhang2025KLEstimators,
  author       = {Wang, Xihuai and Zhang, Shao},
  title        = {Understanding KL Divergence Estimators in RL: From Numerical Estimation to Gradient Estimation},
  year         = {2025},
  month        = dec,
  day          = {01},
  url          = {https://xihuai18.github.io/reinforcement-learning/2025/12/01/kl-estimators-zh.html}
}
entry.summary: 在强化学习中，不同 KL 估计器在训练里的行为可能差别很大。本文比较三种经典估计器 k1、k2、k3 在 on-policy 与 off-policy 场景下的性质差异，并说明当 KL 作为可微 loss 或 detached 的奖励塑形项时，选型结论会怎样变化。

entry.title: From Two Policies to Three: Extending TRPO under Behavior–Reference Policy Mismatch in LLM RL

link.title: From Two Policies to Three: Extending TRPO under Behavior–Reference Policy Mismatch in LLM RL

entry.content:
1. Training–Inference Mismatch and Asynchronous Frameworks

Recent LLM RL work has repeatedly run into the same issue: the behavior policy that actually generates data may not match the reference policy used in training.

This post first reviews recent work, then organizes it around that single mismatch.

Throughout the post I’ll use:

  • Behavior policy ⟦INLMATH48MATHEND⟧: the policy that actually generates rollouts, i.e., “under which distribution your data are sampled.” In modern LLM RL systems this typically corresponds to the implementation inside the inference engine (vLLM, SGLang, etc.), and under asynchronous frameworks it is often a mixture distribution over multiple worker policies.

  • Reference policy ⟦INLMATH49MATHEND⟧: the policy used in the training objective for importance sampling, clipping, or KL constraints — typically the “old policy” in PPO / GRPO.

  • Target policy ⟦INLMATH50MATHEND⟧: the policy we optimize in the training objective, i.e., “what we want the model to become” — typically the “new policy” in PPO / GRPO.

In the classical idealized setup, we usually implicitly assume ⟦INLMATH51MATHEND⟧. In real systems, however, asynchronous updates, different inference / training backends, MoE routing fluctuations, and even hardware-level numerical differences cause these two policies to deviate to varying degrees.

2. Related Work

Below is a rough timeline of the works that left a strong impression on me (this is only a partial and biased subset of the literature I’ve seen):

  • Decoupled PPO was among the first to point out that in trust-region policy optimization methods (TRPO and PPO), the “old policy” actually plays two distinct roles:

    1. It is used for importance sampling to perform off-policy correction. In this sense, the “old policy” is meant to represent the behavior policy that generated the training data.

    2. It is also used to limit the update step size of the new policy. In this sense, the “old policy” acts as a baseline to measure how much the new and old policies differ, i.e., a proximal policy (what I call the reference policy here).

    The paper points out that these two roles do not have to be played by the same policy, and proposes the Decoupled PPO objective, which explicitly decouples “who generates the data” from “who defines the trust region” at the level of the optimization objective.

  • AReaL focuses on the mismatch between behavior and reference policies under asynchronous training frameworks: rollouts are often generated by stale parameter versions or different workers. The paper adopts a Decoupled-PPO-style objective in the asynchronous setting, explicitly separating the behavior distribution from the reference policy, while still maintaining PPO-like optimization properties in this asynchronous regime.

  • GSPO starts from stability issues of GRPO on long sequences and MoE models. It shows that token-level PPO / GRPO can become highly unstable when MoE expert routing is extremely volatile (especially when routing differs significantly between old and new policies), leading to large variance and training collapse. GSPO proposes a sequence-level PPO-style objective and ratio constraint, using the ratio over entire sequences to control updates. This substantially mitigates training collapse in MoE scenarios caused by routing instability and token-level noise.

  • Your Efficient RL Framework Secretly Brings You Off-Policy RL Training observes that in existing LLM RL frameworks (such as VeRL), the inference stack and the training stack often differ across multiple functional modules (e.g., vLLM vs. FSDP / Megatron kernels and operators). This makes the behavior policy ⟦INLMATH52MATHEND⟧ differ from the reference policy ⟦INLMATH53MATHEND⟧, so what is assumed to be on-policy training actually becomes off-policy training with nontrivial bias. The article summarizes two existing ways to handle this: PPO-IS and vanilla-IS, and further proposes token-level truncated importance sampling (TIS) to downweight samples with severe training–inference mismatch. The author also wrote two more foundational notes analyzing training–inference mismatch from basic principles: Part I and Part II.

  • Defeating Nondeterminism in LLM Inference points out that the lack of batch-size invariance is a core source of randomness in LLM inference: the same input can yield noticeably different probability distributions under different batch compositions and kernel paths. This means that even when you “nominally” have a single set of parameters, the behavior policy ⟦INLMATH54MATHEND⟧ realized in practice can fluctuate with system load and scheduling, further exacerbating training–inference mismatch.

  • Small Leak Can Sink a Great Ship—Boost RL Training on MoE with 𝑰𝒄𝒆𝑷𝒐𝒑! observes that the above mismatch issues are further amplified in MoE models: routing itself is highly sensitive to small perturbations, and stacked with inference / training implementation differences and asynchronous sampling, it is easy to magnify bias and instability. The paper proposes IcePop: at the token level, it computes importance sampling ratios and applies two-sided masking to discard tokens whose ratios are either too large or too small. This removes “very noisy” data from the gradient, stabilizing RL training on MoE models.

  • When Speed Kills Stability: Demystifying RL Collapse from the Training-Inference Mismatch gives a systematic analysis of the causes of training–inference mismatch, including large amounts of out-of-distribution and low-probability content introduced by agent workflows, hardware and kernel-level numerical uncertainty, and how token-level importance sampling can introduce severe bias on long sequences. It further proposes sequence-level masked importance sampling (sequence-level MIS): compute an IS ratio at the sequence level and discard only those sequences whose overall ratio is too large, thereby controlling bias while strongly suppressing training collapse caused by extreme samples. The paper provides reasonably complete theoretical derivations and extensive experimental evidence.

  • Stabilizing MoE Reinforcement Learning by Aligning Training and Inference Routers focuses on the MoE-specific problem of routing inconsistency. The paper finds that even for identical inputs, inference and training can route tokens to different experts due to small differences in operator implementations or parallelism. This “physical-path” mismatch makes the gap between the behavior policy ⟦INLMATH55MATHEND⟧ and the reference policy ⟦INLMATH56MATHEND⟧ much larger than expected and can easily cause training collapse. To address this, the paper proposes Rollout Routing Replay (R3): during rollout it records, for each token, the actual expert indices selected by the inference router, and during training it replays these routing decisions instead of recomputing them. In effect, R3 forces the training and inference stacks to share the same routing paths in the MoE topology, aligning the two sides at the level of the computation graph.

  • RL 老训崩？训推差异是基石 approaches the problem more from a practical perspective, sharing experience on how to engineer for near training–inference consistency: choosing consistent operators and precision settings, monitoring and constraining the log-prob gap between training and inference, etc. The focus is on framework-level engineering practices that can mitigate training–inference difference at the root.

  • verl Rollout Importance Sampling introduces a Token Veto mechanism in its rollout correction module: it computes token-level importance ratios ⟦INLMATH57MATHEND⟧, and if any token in a trajectory satisfies ⟦INLMATH58MATHEND⟧, the entire sequence is discarded from training. This “token-level detection, sequence-level veto” design embodies a conservative “one-vote veto” strategy.

  • INTELLECT-3 Technical Report adopts a similar rejection sampling strategy in its asynchronous distributed RL training framework. INTELLECT-3 computes token-level importance ratios for each rollout; if any token’s ratio falls below a threshold (⟦INLMATH59MATHEND⟧ in the paper), the entire trajectory is masked.

3. A Minimally Unified View from a Three-Policy TRPO Perspective

At first glance, the works listed above seem to tackle different aspects:

  • Algorithmic level: how to formulate PPO / GRPO objectives, token-level vs. sequence-level, clip vs. mask, etc.
  • Systems level: how to align inference and training stacks.
  • Model level: how MoE routing amplifies instability, and so on.

However, if we organize everything along a single axis — behavior policy vs. reference policy — most of these issues can be placed inside a single theoretical frame: three-policy TRPO.

The derivation itself is small: take the usual TRPO lower bound and add one triangle inequality. But that extra split is exactly what makes training-inference mismatch easier to reason about:

  • On the one hand, it helps us understand what exactly “training–inference mismatch” and “asynchronous training frameworks” are harming within the TRPO view.
  • On the other hand, it offers a unifying way to interpret TIS, IcePop, sequence-level MIS, etc. In the view of this post, they can all be seen as different incarnations of Constraint 2 introduced below.

3.1 Three Policies

We stick to the notation from above and consider a discounted MDP with discount factor ⟦INLMATH60MATHEND⟧:

For LLM RL, many workloads are closer to finite-horizon sequence decision problems than to infinite-horizon discounted MDPs. I still use the discounted-MDP notation here because it connects most directly to the classical TRPO derivation; the same decomposition carries over to finite-horizon versions as well.

  • Behavior policy ⟦INLMATH64MATHEND⟧: the policy that actually generates rollouts. Data ⟦INLMATH65MATHEND⟧ are sampled from it.

  • Reference policy ⟦INLMATH66MATHEND⟧: the “old policy” used in the optimization objective for importance sampling ratios, clipping, or KL constraints.

  • Target policy ⟦INLMATH67MATHEND⟧: the policy we are optimizing in this update.

In the ideal setup we assume ⟦INLMATH68MATHEND⟧; in real systems they are often unequal. This is the mathematical shadow of “training–inference mismatch.”

If you’re already familiar with TRPO, feel free to skip ahead to the “Three-Policy TRPO” subsection.

In most PPO / GRPO / GSPO / RLHF-style frameworks, the actual situation is:

Strictly speaking, though, the bound by itself does not guarantee improvement unless ⟦INLMATH105MATHEND⟧ is large enough to overcome the penalty term, and in large LLM settings the constant ⟦INLMATH106MATHEND⟧ can be very loose. So the main value of Theorem 2 is structural: it tells us which two mismatch sources we need to track.

3.4 How to Control These Two Deviations in Practice?

We can now revisit various practical methods through the lens of Theorem 2:

  • Most PPO / GRPO / GSPO-style work focuses on controlling Constraint 1: ⟦INLMATH107MATHEND⟧.
  • Most TIS / IcePop / MIS-style work, in the view of this post, can be understood as primarily targeting Constraint 2: ⟦INLMATH108MATHEND⟧.

In the remainder of this post I will focus on Constraint 2.

The goal of Constraint 2 is: ensure that the data used in training come (effectively) from a behavior policy that is close to the reference policy.

In practice, this usually involves both system-level mechanisms and algorithmic mechanisms (importance sampling).

  1. System level: keep the behavior policy from drifting too far

    • Asynchronous frameworks: Tag each sample with a policy version, and only use data generated by parameter versions that are close enough to ⟦INLMATH109MATHEND⟧.

    • Training–inference alignment: Use consistent precision, operators, and similar kernel behavior between the training and inference stacks.

    These mechanisms act “outside” the algorithm to make ⟦INLMATH110MATHEND⟧ closer to ⟦INLMATH111MATHEND⟧, thereby shrinking ⟦INLMATH112MATHEND⟧.

  2. Algorithmic level: sample-wise correction

    At the algorithmic level, we no longer attempt to “fix” the entire behavior policy. Instead, we use importance sampling ratios to correct at the sample level: we filter or reweight samples so that the behavior policy is close to the reference policy on the subset of data that actually participates in training, or at least reduce the influence of samples with large mismatch.

    Concretely, this gives rise to methods like TIS, IcePop, and MIS, which can be seen as different ways of implementing Constraint 2 at the sample level.

The core operation in sequence-level MIS is to retain only sequences whose sequence-level IS ratio is below a threshold ⟦INLMATH131MATHEND⟧, zeroing out the loss for all other sequences:

The verl Token Veto mechanism and INTELLECT-3 both adopt a rejection sampling strategy that can be collectively called Worst Token Reject Sampling (WTRS):
